title: Unit 03 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Branches of Government and Checks and Balances.

questions.prompt: Which branch of government makes national laws?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. The executive branch carries out and enforces laws.
Correct. The legislative branch makes national laws through Congress.
Incorrect. The judicial branch interprets laws.
Incorrect. There is no separate cabinet branch of government.

questions.prompt: The president leads the executive branch.

choices.feedback: Correct. The president is the head of the executive branch.
Incorrect. The president does lead the executive branch.

questions.prompt: What is the main job of the judicial branch?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Enforcing laws is the role of the executive branch.
Incorrect. Making laws is the role of the legislative branch.
Correct. The judicial branch interprets laws through the courts.
Incorrect. Citizens elect representatives; that is not the judicial branch’s role.

questions.prompt: Congress is part of the legislative branch.

choices.feedback: Correct. Congress is the legislative branch of the national government.
Incorrect. Congress is part of the legislative branch.

questions.prompt: Which branch is most directly responsible for carrying out and enforcing laws?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. The judicial branch interprets laws.
Incorrect. The legislative branch makes laws.
Correct. The executive branch carries out and enforces laws.
Incorrect. There is no national branch called the state branch.

questions.prompt: What does judicial review allow courts to do?

choices.text: Decide whether a law follows the Constitution
choices.feedback: Correct. Judicial review allows courts to determine whether laws are constitutional.
Incorrect. Courts do not write laws.
Incorrect. Courts do not elect the president.
Incorrect. That is not the role of judicial review.

questions.prompt: Checks and balances helps prevent one branch of government from becoming too powerful.

choices.feedback: Correct. Checks and balances allows branches to limit one another.
Incorrect. Preventing too much power in one branch is the purpose of checks and balances.

questions.prompt: Which situation best shows the legislative branch in action?

choices.text: A court reviews whether a law is constitutional
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the judicial branch.

choices.text: The president signs and enforces a law
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the executive branch.

choices.text: Congress debates and votes on a bill
choices.feedback: Correct. Debating and voting on bills is part of the lawmaking role of the legislative branch.

choices.text: Judges interpret what a law means in a court case
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the judicial branch.
